title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s signal is heavy on evaluation limits and robustness gaps rather than new deployment rules. The strongest items sharpen how we should audit rare failures, measure model behavior under brittle constraints, benchmark policy and multimodal understanding, and gate high-capability cyber access with tighter safeguards.

items.title: OpenAI expands trusted cyber access program with GPT-5.4-Cyber
items.why_it_matters: Controlled access and stronger safeguards shape how high-risk cyber capabilities are deployed to defenders.

items.title: The Verification Tax analyzes fundamental limits of auditing rare AI errors
items.why_it_matters: It argues rare-error auditing faces hard sample-complexity limits, affecting assurance, calibration, and safety claims.

items.title: PolicyLLM introduces PolicyBench for evaluating LLM comprehension of public policy
items.why_it_matters: Policy-specific benchmarks matter as models are used in governance, compliance, and public-sector decision support.

items.title: One Token Away from Collapse finds instruction-tuned helpfulness can be brittle
items.why_it_matters: Small lexical constraints causing large performance drops raise concerns about robustness and dependable deployment.

items.title: Survey maps hallucination risks in video language models
items.why_it_matters: A consolidated taxonomy and benchmark view can improve multimodal evaluation and mitigation of false video-grounded claims.

items.title: LogicEval proposes a framework to evaluate automated repair of logical software vulnerabilities
items.why_it_matters: Better evaluation of logic-bug repair is relevant to securing AI-assisted coding and vulnerability remediation.

items.title: Parallax argues AI agents that reason should be separated from systems that act
items.why_it_matters: The paper centers architectural containment and permission boundaries for agent safety.

items.title: Continual face forgery detection paper targets forgetting as new manipulation methods emerge
items.why_it_matters: Improving persistent deepfake detection supports media integrity as attack techniques evolve over time.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
